title: Chapter 23: Five Minutes
content: Feng took the token in the garden at dusk, his hands steady despite the tremor that had become his constant companion.
"It'll burn," Ren warned. "The suppressant pulse is designed to shock the entity—not your nervous system, but you'll feel it. Like sticking your hand in ice water."

"I've felt worse." Feng's voice was matter-of-fact. He'd moved beyond fear in the past weeks—not because the fear was gone, but because he'd compressed it into something harder, denser, more useful. "Walk me through it again."

"Enter the restricted wing through the ceremony hall door—the array will accept your bloodline signature with no questions. Proceed to the junction chamber. The Bell is on the central pedestal. Place the flat side of the token against the Bell's rim and hold it for thirty seconds. The token will deliver two payloads: the maintenance frequency injection to continue the containment drift, and the suppressant pulse to keep the entity dormant."
"Thirty seconds."
"Count slowly. One-Mississippi pace. Don't rush—if you pull away too early, the injection won't complete."
Feng nodded. He turned the token over in his palm—a small rectangle of ancient jade, warm against his skin, thrumming with the imprint of the containment frequency.
"And if the entity breaks through?"

"Get out. Drop the token if you have to—it's attuned to you now, so the array will register it as yours. The Patriarch might find it, but he'll assume you were trying to channel the Bell on his orders. Plausible deniability."
"Plausible deniability." Feng's lips twitched. "You sound like a spy."

"I was a soldier. Then I died. Then I came back and became a husband, a consultant, a double agent, and whatever this is." Ren gestured at the garden, the estate, the sky above. "War doesn't care about job titles."

"No. It doesn't." Feng's expression shifted—softer, more vulnerable, the younger brother showing through the soldier's mask he was trying to wear. "Ren. If this doesn't work—if the entity takes me during the operation and I... if I'm not me anymore—"
"Then I'll still save you. Or I'll die trying." Ren gripped his shoulder. "That's a promise. Not a soldier's promise, not a strategist's calculation. Just a promise from one person to another."
Feng swallowed hard. Then he nodded and pocketed the token.
"How long do I have? After the suppressant wears off?"
"Five minutes of full lucidity. After that, the entity starts pushing back. You'll have maybe two more minutes of contested consciousness before it takes full control."
"Seven minutes total. In and out in five."
"That's the plan."
"Plans don't survive contact with the enemy."
"Then improvise. But improvise *quickly*."

Feng almost smiled. Then he walked back toward the estate, his figure growing smaller against the golden windows, carrying in his pocket a dead man's artifact and the last best hope of preventing his sister's murder.

The operation was scheduled for the following night—Thursday, when the Patriarch held his weekly strategy session in the west wing and the restricted wing's skeleton crew was at minimum staffing. Feng would enter during the guard rotation gap, timed to the minute using the patrol schedule Ren had memorized weeks ago.

Ren waited in his room with the Salvation System's interface monitoring the estate's ambient array signature for any anomalies. Xue was in her study, deliberately visible on the household surveillance, establishing an alibi for both of them.
At 10:47 PM, the system detected a bloodline signature entering the restricted wing.

`SUBJECT: Liang Feng`
`ZONE: Ceremony Hall — Black Iron Door`
`AUTHENTICATION: Bloodline key accepted`
`STATUS: Proceeding to junction chamber`
Green indicators. Feng was moving.

`10:49 PM — Subject has passed the first checkpoint`
`10:51 PM — Subject approaching the junction chamber`
`10:52 PM — SUBJECT HAS ENTERED THE JUNCTION CHAMBER`
Ren held his breath. The token's imprinted frequency was a one-shot—if the injection didn't take, they'd have to find another way to increase the Bell's containment drift before the demonstration.

`10:52:30 — Token activated. Maintenance frequency injection in progress.`
`10:53:00 — Injection at 50% completion. Suppressant pulse charged.`
`10:53:15 — SUPPRESSANT PULSE DELIVERED`
The Salvation System flared with data:

`🟢 SUPPRESSANT: EFFECTIVE`
`ENTITY RESPONSE: Immediate dormancy onset. The parasitic consciousness has been forced into a reduced-activity state.`
`HOST STATUS: Full lucidity restored. Estimated duration: 5 minutes.`
Feng was clear. The entity was down. He had five minutes to complete the injection and get out.

`10:54:00 — Injection at 80%`
`10:54:30 — Injection at 90%`
Something changed.

The ambient array signature—the constant, low-level hum of the dimensional network beneath the estate—*spiked*. Not a gradual increase, but a sudden, violent surge that made Ren's enhanced senses flare with warning.

`⚠️ ANOMALY DETECTED`
`ARRAY ACTIVITY: Critical spike in Node 5 (Bronze Bell) resonance`
`CAUSE: The injection frequency has interacted with an existing calibration protocol in an UNEXPECTED WAY`
`RESULT: The Bell is resonating at a frequency that's creating sympathetic vibration in Node 2 (Patriarch's Seal) and Node 6 (Obsidian Tablet)`

*Sympathetic vibration.* The maintenance frequency was designed to drift the Bell toward containment—but the other nodes were *resonating* with it, like tuning forks vibrating in sympathy. The containment frequency was *spreading*.

`ASSESSMENT: The Bell's drift is accelerating beyond projected parameters.`
`Current drift: 0.6% → 1.4%`
`Projected drift at calibration end: 2.3% (above the 2% sabotage threshold)`
`ADDITIONAL EFFECT: Node 2 (Patriarch's Seal) is experiencing minor resonance interference (0.1% containment drift)`
`CRITICAL: This interference will be detectable by the Patriarch's monitoring systems within 24 hours.`

The sabotage was working *too well*. The Bell had absorbed the injection so efficiently that its containment drift was spreading to adjacent nodes—a cascade effect that the original practitioners had probably designed into the system as a self-repair mechanism.
*The containment array wants to heal itself,* Ren realized. *It's been damaged for twenty years, and now that it's getting a taste of restoration, it's trying to pull the other nodes back too.*

Which was wonderful for the long game—and catastrophic for the short one. If the Patriarch detected a containment drift in his own Seal, he'd know exactly what was happening. He'd lock down the restricted wing, search every node, and Feng would be caught with a jade token in his pocket.
*Get out,* Ren thought urgently. *Feng, get out NOW.*
At 10:55 PM, the system detected Feng leaving the junction chamber. Moving fast—not running, but close to it, the stride of a man who knew his five-minute window was shrinking.

`10:56 PM — Subject has left the junction chamber`
`10:57 PM — Subject approaching the Black Iron Door`
`10:57:30 — ENTITY RESURFACING`
The suppressant was wearing off.

`HOST STATUS: Lucidity degrading. Entity consciousness reasserting at 15%.`
`SUBJECT MOVEMENT: Still controlled by host. But deteriorating.`

`10:58 PM — Subject has exited the restricted wing`
`10:58:30 — Entity consciousness at 35%. Host losing fine motor control.`
`10:59 PM — ENTITY CONSCIOUSNESS AT 60%. HOST BEING OVERRIDDEN.`
Feng was in the ceremony hall, less than fifty meters from his own quarters. But the entity was coming back fast—faster than projected, as if the suppressant had *angered* it.
`11:00 PM — ENTITY DOMINANT. HOST CONSCIOUSNESS SUBMERGED.`
The Salvation System's tracking followed Feng's bloodline signature as it changed trajectory. Instead of heading toward his room, Feng-who-wasn't-Feng was walking toward the Patriarch's wing.
*Toward the Patriarch.*
"Ren!" Xue's voice came from the doorway. She'd felt the array spike—her channeling training had made her sensitive to dimensional fluctuations. "What's happening?"
"Feng's been taken. The entity broke through faster than expected." Ren was already moving, heading for the door. "It's going to the Patriarch."
"Then we have to stop it."
"We can't fight a Stage 2 spore host. Not with 1.3% power."

"Then we don't fight. We *redirect*." Xue grabbed his arm. "The entity doesn't want the Patriarch—it wants the Portal. If the Patriarch knows that the entity is walking toward him inside his own house, he'll respond. And a Patriarch responding to a threat is a Patriarch who's not monitoring his array diagnostics."

She was right. The array spike from the cascade effect would be visible in the Patriarch's monitoring systems—but only if he was looking. If he was busy dealing with a possessed son walking through his front door, he wouldn't be checking node diagnostics.
"Feng is heading for the west wing. The Patriarch's study is there. If the entity reaches Tianming—"

"It won't attack him. It needs him too much—without the Patriarch, there's no one to complete the portal conversion." Xue's eyes were sharp. "The entity will *confront* him. Demand something. Accelerations, protections, changes to the plan. And while they're arguing, the cascade will be spreading."

The strategic insight was clean, cold, and correct. The entity wasn't the Patriarch's enemy—it was his partner. And partners argued. The argument would buy time—time for the containment cascade to spread through the node network before the Patriarch noticed.
"We need to make sure that argument lasts at least two hours," Ren said. "The cascade needs time to infiltrate Node 2's conversion protocols."

"I can help with that." Xue touched the jade pendant. "If I channel a containment pulse from outside the restricted wing—just enough to irritate the entity without triggering the firewall—it'll make the entity more aggressive. More demanding. A more aggressive negotiator makes for a longer negotiation."
"You'd be revealing your channeling ability to the entity."

"I'd be revealing it to something that already knows I have it. The entity can sense bloodline activity, remember?" Xue straightened. "I'm going to the garden. You're going to the library, where the cameras will see you, where you'll have an alibi, and where the Salvation System can monitor the cascade without being detected."
"Xue—"

"Don't." Her voice was firm. "Don't tell me to be careful. Don't tell me it's dangerous. I know exactly what I'm doing, and I'm doing it anyway." She paused at the door. "My mother did the same thing, twelve years ago. She channeled alone in this house, hiding from my father, fighting a war no one else could see. She lost. I won't."
Then she was gone.
Ren went to the library. Sat in his usual chair. Opened a book he wouldn't read.
The Salvation System monitored the estate in real-time:

`11:03 PM — Entity (in Feng's body) has reached the Patriarch's study`
`11:04 PM — Confrontation initiated. Entity is demanding acceleration of Node 6 integration.`
`11:05 PM — Xue is channeling from the garden. Containment pulse at 2% intensity.`
`11:06 PM — Entity response: heightened aggression. Demands escalating.`
`11:07 PM — Patriarch is attempting to de-escalate. Not succeeding.`

The cascade was spreading. Node 2, the Patriarch's Seal, was drifting by 0.15% toward containment. Not enough to trigger alarms—not yet. But more than enough to prove that the self-repair mechanism was real, and that the containment array *wanted* to be whole.

`11:45 PM — CONTAINMENT CASCADE STATUS:`
`Node 2 drift: 0.22% (increasing)`
`Node 5 drift: 1.9% (approaching threshold)`
`Node 6 resonance: Beginning to show sympathetic interference (0.05% drift)`

`12:15 AM — Entity has ended confrontation with Patriarch. Retreating to Feng's quarters.`
`12:16 AM — Xue has stopped channeling. En route to her room.`
`12:17 AM — Patriarch is... not checking array diagnostics. He's consulting with his security team about the Feng incident.`
*It worked.*

The containment cascade was spreading, the sabotage threshold had been exceeded, and the Patriarch was focused on the wrong threat. He'd had a possessed son walk into his study and make demands—and that, not a 0.22% drift in his Seal's resonance, was what occupied his attention.

By the time he thought to check the array diagnostics, the Bell's drift would be past 2%. The demonstration triangle wouldn't form. The aperture would fail. And Zhao Kang would walk away from the deal for good.
Ren closed the book and leaned back in his chair.
*Two operations. Two successes. The War God is 1.3% of what he was, and he's already won two battles.*
The countdown pulsed:
`1068 days`
But the real victory wasn't the days. It was the drift.
Node 5 was drifting toward containment. Node 2 was starting to follow. Node 6 was showing the first signs of sympathetic interference.
The seal was healing.
Slowly. Imperceptibly. One fraction of a percent at a time.
But healing.
And in forty-four days, the Patriarch's demonstration was going to fail.
